---
title: "DNA Shield Proteomics — CP34H (*Colwellia psychrerythraea* 34H)"
subtitle: "PSM-based analysis of control vs. three DNA Shield dilutions"
author: "Evelyn Bartlett — Brook Lab"
date: "`r format(Sys.Date(), '%B %d, %Y')`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    toc_float: true
    code_folding: show
    df_print: paged
  pdf_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo       = TRUE,
  warning    = FALSE,
  message    = FALSE,
  fig.path   = "figures/",
  dev        = "png",
  dpi        = 600,
  fig.width  = 7,
  fig.height = 4.5
)
```

# Overview

This notebook analyzes Comet PSM output for CP34H (*Colwellia psychrerythraea* 34H)
across four conditions (n = 3 each):

| Group | Label              | Description                       |
|-------|--------------------|-----------------------------------|
| BC    | Control            | No DNA Shield                     |
| E     | DNA Shield 1:1.5   | 1:1.5 dilution                    |
| F     | DNA Shield 1:1.75  | 1:1.75 dilution                   |
| G     | DNA Shield 1:2     | 1:2 dilution                      |

The analysis (a) flags common-contaminant proteins for verification,
(b) computes per-group PSM means / SDs, (c) runs Welch t-tests with BH-FDR
for each treatment-vs-control comparison, and (d) produces Nature-quality
600 DPI PNG figures.

## Outputs index

Every figure and every CSV produced by this notebook is listed below with
the chunk that generates it.

**Figures** (saved to `figures/`, all 600 DPI PNG):

| File                                | Chunk             | Section                                      |
|-------------------------------------|-------------------|----------------------------------------------|
| `fig1_sample_QC.png`                | `qc`              | Figure 1 — Sample-level QC (PSMs + peptides) |
| `fig1_sample_QC_with_significance.png`| `qc`            | Figure 1 — Same, with Welch t-test brackets  |
| `fig1_peptides_only.png`            | `qc`              | Figure 1 (peptides only, standalone)         |
| `fig1_peptides_only_with_significance.png` | `qc`         | Figure 1 (peptides only) with brackets       |
| `fig2_PCA.png`                      | `pca`             | Figure 2 — PCA on log2 PSM counts            |
| `fig3_sample_correlation.png`       | `corr_heatmap`    | Figure 3 — Sample-sample correlation heatmap |
| `fig4_volcano.png`                  | `volcano`         | Figure 4 — Volcano plots vs control          |
| `fig5_top_variable_heatmap.png`     | `heatmap_top`     | Figure 5 — Top variable proteins heatmap     |
| `fig6_total_PSMs_by_group.png`      | `boxplot`         | Figure 6 — Total PSM yield by condition      |
| `fig7_NMDS.png`                     | `nmds`            | Figure 7 — NMDS on Bray-Curtis dissimilarity |
| `fig8_peptide_PSM_scatter.png`              | `peptide_scatter` | Figure 8 — Per-peptide PSM scatter, control vs each dilution (KEGG-style coloring) |
| `fig8_peptide_PSM_scatter_with_errorbars.png` | `peptide_scatter` | Figure 8b — Same scatter with SEM error bars                                       |
| `fig9_modifications.png`                    | `modifications`   | Figure 9 — Top peptide modifications by PSM count (grouped bars per condition)     |
| `fig11_NSAF_scatter.png`                    | `nsaf_scatter`    | Figure 11 — Per-protein NSAF scatter, control vs each dilution (KEGG-style colors) |
| `fig11_NSAF_scatter_with_errorbars.png`     | `nsaf_scatter`    | Figure 11b — Same scatter with SEM error bars                                      |
| `fig10_peptide_venn.png`                    | `peptide_overlap` | Figure 10 — 4-way Venn of peptide overlap across conditions (counts + %)           |
| `peptide_filtered/fig1_sample_QC.png`       | `peptide_filtered`| Figure 1 (singletons removed) — sample QC, peptide ≥ 2 PSMs total                  |
| `peptide_filtered/fig3_sample_correlation.png` | `peptide_filtered` | Figure 3 (singletons removed) — peptide-level correlation heatmap               |
| `peptide_filtered/fig7_NMDS.png`            | `peptide_filtered`| Figure 7 (singletons removed) — peptide-level NMDS                                 |
| `peptide_filtered/fig10_peptide_venn.png`   | `peptide_filtered`| Figure 10 (singletons removed) — 4-way Venn                                        |

**Data tables** (CSVs saved to the project folder):

| File                            | Chunk            | Section                                                       |
|---------------------------------|------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------|
| `contaminants_to_verify.csv`    | `contaminants`   | Contaminant flagging                                          |
| `proteins_clean.csv`            | `clean_matrix`   | Build clean protein matrix                                    |
| `group_summary.csv`             | `summary_stats`  | Group-wise summary statistics (mean / SD per group)           |
| `differential_abundance.csv`    | `diff_abundance` | Differential abundance — Welch t-test + BH-FDR vs control     |
| `dose_response_trends.csv`      | `dose_response`  | Dose-response across the dilution gradient (Spearman ρ)       |

# Setup

```{r packages}
# Core
library(tidyverse)
library(stringr)
library(readr)

# Plotting
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)
library(scales)
library(viridis)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(patchwork)
library(pheatmap)

# Stats
# (uses base R — t.test + p.adjust)

# Pathing
in_dir   <- "."   # input file lives in the project root
fig_dir  <- "figures"
dir.create(fig_dir, showWarnings = FALSE)
```

# Configuration

```{r config}
proteins_file <- file.path(in_dir, "Bactera_Comet_all_samples_proteins.txt")

samples <- list(
  BC = c("HR_BC1", "HR_BC2", "HR_BC3"),
  E  = c("HR-E1",  "HR_E2",  "HR_E3"),    # 1:1.5
  F  = c("HR_F1",  "HR-F2",  "HR_F3"),    # 1:1.75
  G  = c("HR_G1",  "HR_G2",  "HR_G3")     # 1:2
)
group_labels <- c(BC = "CP34H Control",
                  E  = "DRS 1:1.5",
                  F  = "DRS 1:1.75",
                  G  = "DRS 1:2")
group_order <- c("BC", "E", "F", "G")

palette_cols <- c(
  BC = "#4D4D4D",   # control — neutral dark grey, anchors the figure
  E  = "#AED6F1",   # 1:1.5  — light blue
  F  = "#3498DB",   # 1:1.75 — medium blue
  G  = "#1B4F72"    # 1:2    — deep blue
)
# Poster palette for the CP34H arm: blue family.
# Treatment intensity is encoded by blue darkness; control sits outside
# the gradient as a neutral baseline.

# Nature-quality theme — 8 pt sans, no chart-junk
theme_nature <- function(base_size = 8) {
  theme_classic(base_size = base_size, base_family = "sans") +
  theme(
    axis.line       = element_line(linewidth = 0.4),
    axis.ticks      = element_line(linewidth = 0.4),
    axis.text       = element_text(color = "black"),
    plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0,
                                   size = base_size + 1),
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.key.size = unit(0.4, "cm"),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text       = element_text(face = "bold")
  )
}

# High-DPI PNG saver — call after every figure
save_png <- function(plot, name, w = 7, h = 4.5, dpi = 600) {
  ggsave(file.path(fig_dir, paste0(name, ".png")),
         plot = plot, width = w, height = h, units = "in",
         dpi = dpi, bg = "white", type = "cairo")
  invisible(plot)
}
```

# Load & tidy

```{r load}
raw <- read_tsv(proteins_file, show_col_types = FALSE)

# PSM and NSAF column names
all_samples <- unname(unlist(samples))
psm_cols    <- paste0("PSMs (",  all_samples, ")")
nsaf_cols   <- paste0("NSAF (", all_samples, ")")

# Some PSM columns import as character because of comma thousand-separators.
to_num <- function(x) {
  if (is.character(x)) as.numeric(gsub(",", "", x, fixed = TRUE)) else x
}
raw <- raw %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(c(psm_cols, nsaf_cols)), to_num))

cat("Loaded", nrow(raw), "protein groups,",
    length(psm_cols), "PSM columns\n")
```

# Contaminant flagging  →  `contaminants_to_verify.csv`

We flag common MS contaminants based on protein-description keywords —
keratins, trypsin, BSA/serum albumin, hemoglobin, casein, dermcidin,
desmosomal skin proteins, and explicit human/eukaryotic carry-over —
**unless** the entry is also annotated as *Colwellia* / *psychrerythraea* /
strain 34H, in which case it stays.

```{r contaminants}
contam_patterns <- tribble(
  ~regex,                                            ~reason,
  "(?i)\\bkeratin\\b",                               "keratin",
  "(?i)\\bhuman\\b",                                 "human protein",
  "(?i)homo sapiens|sapiens",                        "human (Homo sapiens)",
  "(?i)trypsin",                                     "trypsin (digestion enzyme)",
  "(?i)\\bBSA\\b|serum albumin|albumin",             "albumin / BSA",
  "(?i)bovine",                                      "bovine carry-over",
  "(?i)\\bsheep\\b|\\bovis\\b",                      "sheep carry-over",
  "(?i)\\bpig\\b|\\bporcine\\b|sus scrofa",          "porcine carry-over",
  "(?i)\\bchicken\\b|gallus",                        "chicken carry-over",
  "(?i)\\bmouse\\b|\\brat\\b|mus musculus",          "rodent contaminant",
  "(?i)hornerin|filaggrin|involucrin|loricrin",      "skin protein",
  "(?i)hemoglobin",                                  "hemoglobin (blood)",
  "(?i)casein",                                      "casein (milk)",
  "(?i)streptavidin",                                "streptavidin",
  "(?i)^cRAP|CON__",                                 "cRAP/contaminant DB tag",
  "(?i)dermcidin",                                   "skin/sweat dermcidin",
  "(?i)desmoplakin|desmoglein|corneodesmosin",       "skin desmosomal"
)

whitelist_re <- regex("colwellia|psychrerythraea|34H", ignore_case = TRUE)

flag_one <- function(desc, prot) {
  txt <- paste(desc, prot, sep = " || ")
  if (str_detect(txt, whitelist_re)) return(list(flag = "", reason = ""))
  hits <- contam_patterns$reason[str_detect(txt, contam_patterns$regex)]
  if (length(hits) == 0) list(flag = "", reason = "")
  else list(flag = "YES", reason = paste(unique(hits), collapse = "; "))
}

flag_df <- map2_dfr(raw$`Protein Description(s)`, raw$`Protein(s)`, flag_one)
raw$contaminant_flag   <- flag_df$flag
raw$contaminant_reason <- flag_df$reason

contam <- raw %>%
  filter(contaminant_flag == "YES") %>%
  mutate(total_PSMs_all_samples = rowSums(across(all_of(psm_cols)), na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  arrange(desc(total_PSMs_all_samples))

cat("Flagged", nrow(contam), "contaminant candidates\n")

contam %>%
  select(`Protein(s)`, `Protein Description(s)`, contaminant_reason,
         total_PSMs_all_samples, all_of(psm_cols), `Protein Group Number`) %>%
  write_csv("contaminants_to_verify.csv")
```

# Build clean protein matrix  →  `proteins_clean.csv`

```{r clean_matrix}
clean <- raw %>% filter(contaminant_flag != "YES")
cat("Clean protein set:", nrow(clean),
    "(removed", nrow(raw) - nrow(clean), "contaminants)\n")

psm_mat <- clean %>%
  select(all_of(psm_cols)) %>%
  rename_with(~ all_samples) %>%        # short names: HR_BC1, ...
  mutate(across(everything(), ~ replace_na(.x, 0)))
rownames(psm_mat) <- clean$`Protein(s)`
psm_mat <- as.matrix(psm_mat)

write_csv(
  clean %>% select(`Protein(s)`, `Protein Description(s)`,
                   all_of(psm_cols), all_of(nsaf_cols)),
  "proteins_clean.csv"
)
```

# Figure 1 — Sample-level QC  →  `fig1_sample_QC.png`

```{r qc}
# Peptide counts come from the peptides file (not the proteins file).
# Count = number of peptide sequences with PSM Count > 0 in each sample.
peptides_file <- file.path(in_dir, "Bacteria_Comet_all_samples_peptides.txt")
pep <- read_tsv(peptides_file, show_col_types = FALSE)
pep_psm_cols <- paste0("PSM Count (", all_samples, ")")
pep <- pep %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(pep_psm_cols),
                ~ if (is.character(.x))
                    as.numeric(gsub(",", "", .x, fixed = TRUE)) else .x))
n_peptides <- sapply(pep_psm_cols, function(c) sum(pep[[c]] > 0, na.rm = TRUE))
names(n_peptides) <- all_samples

qc <- tibble(
  sample = all_samples,
  group  = rep(group_order, each = 3),
  total_PSMs = colSums(psm_mat),
  n_peptides = n_peptides[all_samples]
) %>% mutate(group = factor(group, levels = group_order))

# Per-group mean and SEM for fig 1 — replicate-collapsed view
qc_grp <- qc %>%
  group_by(group) %>%
  summarize(
    psm_mean  = mean(total_PSMs),
    psm_sem   = sd(total_PSMs)   / sqrt(n()),
    pep_mean  = mean(n_peptides),
    pep_sem   = sd(n_peptides)   / sqrt(n()),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

library(ggsignif)
# Welch's t-test, control vs each treatment.  ns / * / ** / *** / ****
sig_comparisons <- list(c("BC", "E"), c("BC", "F"), c("BC", "G"))

# ---- Build clean (no significance brackets) panels ----
p1_clean <- ggplot(qc_grp, aes(group, psm_mean, fill = group)) +
  geom_col(color = "black", linewidth = 0.4, width = 0.7) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = psm_mean - psm_sem, ymax = psm_mean + psm_sem),
                width = 0.18, linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_jitter(data = qc, aes(group, total_PSMs),
              width = 0.10, shape = 21, fill = "white",
              color = "black", stroke = 0.4, size = 1.6, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = palette_cols, labels = group_labels, guide = "none") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = group_labels) +
  labs(title = "a  Total PSMs per sample",
       x = NULL, y = "Total PSMs (mean ± SEM)") +
  theme_nature() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 20, hjust = 1))

p2_clean <- ggplot(qc_grp, aes(group, pep_mean, fill = group)) +
  geom_col(color = "black", linewidth = 0.4, width = 0.7) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = pep_mean - pep_sem, ymax = pep_mean + pep_sem),
                width = 0.18, linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_jitter(data = qc, aes(group, n_peptides),
              width = 0.10, shape = 21, fill = "white",
              color = "black", stroke = 0.4, size = 1.6, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = palette_cols, labels = group_labels, guide = "none") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = group_labels) +
  labs(title = "b  Peptides detected (PSM > 0)",
       x = NULL, y = "Peptides detected (mean ± SEM)") +
  theme_nature() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 20, hjust = 1))

fig1 <- p1_clean + p2_clean
fig1
save_png(fig1, "fig1_sample_QC", w = 6.5, h = 3.5)

# ---- Build with-significance variant by adding geom_signif() ----
sig_layer <- function(yvar) {
  geom_signif(data = qc, aes(group, !!sym(yvar)),
              comparisons = sig_comparisons,
              test = "t.test", test.args = list(var.equal = FALSE),
              map_signif_level = TRUE, step_increase = 0.08,
              tip_length = 0.01, vjust = 0.2, textsize = 2.6,
              inherit.aes = FALSE)
}
p1_sig <- p1_clean + sig_layer("total_PSMs")
p2_sig <- p2_clean + sig_layer("n_peptides")

fig1_sig <- p1_sig + p2_sig
fig1_sig
save_png(fig1_sig, "fig1_sample_QC_with_significance", w = 7.0, h = 4.0)

# ---- Standalone "peptides-only" figures (clean + with significance) ----
p2_clean_solo <- p2_clean +
  labs(title = "Peptides detected per sample (PSM > 0)")
p2_sig_solo   <- p2_sig +
  labs(title = "Peptides detected per sample (PSM > 0)")

save_png(p2_clean_solo, "fig1_peptides_only",                   w = 3.6, h = 3.5)
save_png(p2_sig_solo,   "fig1_peptides_only_with_significance", w = 3.8, h = 4.0)
```

# Figure 6 — Total PSM yield by condition (boxplot)  →  `fig6_total_PSMs_by_group.png`

```{r boxplot}
fig6 <- ggplot(qc, aes(group, total_PSMs, fill = group)) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.55, alpha = 0.55, color = "black",
               outlier.shape = NA, linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.08, size = 2, shape = 21,
              color = "black", stroke = 0.3) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = palette_cols, guide = "none") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = group_labels) +
  labs(title = "Total PSM yield by condition",
       x = NULL, y = "Total PSMs per sample") +
  theme_nature() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 20, hjust = 1))
fig6
save_png(fig6, "fig6_total_PSMs_by_group", w = 4, h = 3.5)
```

# Figure 2 — PCA on log2 PSM counts  →  `fig2_PCA.png`

```{r pca}
log_mat <- log2(psm_mat + 1)
pca <- prcomp(t(log_mat), center = TRUE, scale. = FALSE)
var_exp <- (pca$sdev^2) / sum(pca$sdev^2)

pca_df <- as_tibble(pca$x[, 1:2], rownames = "sample") %>%
  mutate(group = rep(group_order, each = 3),
         group = factor(group, levels = group_order))

fig2 <- ggplot(pca_df, aes(PC1, PC2, fill = group)) +
  geom_point(size = 3.2, shape = 21, color = "black", stroke = 0.4) +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = sample), size = 2.4, box.padding = 0.25) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = palette_cols, labels = group_labels, name = NULL) +
  labs(title = "PCA of log2 PSM counts",
       x = sprintf("PC1 (%.1f%%)", var_exp[1]*100),
       y = sprintf("PC2 (%.1f%%)", var_exp[2]*100)) +
  theme_nature()
fig2
save_png(fig2, "fig2_PCA", w = 4.5, h = 3.5)
```

# Figure 7 — NMDS on Bray-Curtis dissimilarity  →  `fig7_NMDS.png`

NMDS (non-metric multidimensional scaling) is a rank-based ordination
that, unlike PCA, preserves only the rank-order of pairwise
dissimilarities. It pairs naturally with **Bray-Curtis** for spectral
count data (bounded 0–1, ignores joint absences, robust to many zeros).
Stress < 0.05 is excellent, < 0.10 good, < 0.20 acceptable.

```{r nmds}
# vegan::metaMDS = standard tool. Two trymax retries handle local minima.
library(vegan)
psm_for_bc <- t(psm_mat)                 # samples in rows
set.seed(42)
nmds_fit <- metaMDS(psm_for_bc, distance = "bray", k = 2,
                    trymax = 50, autotransform = FALSE,
                    trace = FALSE)
nmds_df <- as_tibble(nmds_fit$points, rownames = "sample") %>%
  mutate(group = rep(group_order, each = 3),
         group = factor(group, levels = group_order))

fig7 <- ggplot(nmds_df, aes(MDS1, MDS2, fill = group)) +
  geom_point(size = 3.2, shape = 21, color = "black", stroke = 0.4) +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = sample), size = 2.4, box.padding = 0.25) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = palette_cols, labels = group_labels, name = NULL) +
  labs(title = sprintf("NMDS (Bray-Curtis)  —  stress = %.3f", nmds_fit$stress),
       x = "NMDS 1", y = "NMDS 2") +
  theme_nature()
fig7
save_png(fig7, "fig7_NMDS", w = 4.5, h = 3.5)
cat(sprintf("NMDS stress = %.4f  (n iterations: %d)\n",
            nmds_fit$stress, nmds_fit$tries))
```

# Figure 3 — Sample-sample correlation heatmap  →  `fig3_sample_correlation.png`

```{r corr_heatmap}
corr <- cor(log_mat, method = "pearson")
png(file.path(fig_dir, "fig3_sample_correlation.png"),
    width = 5, height = 4.5, units = "in", res = 600, type = "cairo")
pheatmap::pheatmap(
  corr, display_numbers = TRUE, number_format = "%.2f",
  color = viridis::viridis(100),
  fontsize = 7, fontsize_number = 5,
  main = "Sample-sample correlation (log2 PSM)",
  border_color = NA
)
invisible(dev.off())
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(fig_dir, "fig3_sample_correlation.png"))
```

# Group-wise summary statistics  →  `group_summary.csv`

```{r summary_stats}
mean_by_group <- function(g) rowMeans(psm_mat[, samples[[g]], drop = FALSE])
sd_by_group   <- function(g) apply(psm_mat[, samples[[g]], drop = FALSE], 1, sd)

summary_tbl <- tibble(
  protein     = clean$`Protein(s)`,
  description = clean$`Protein Description(s)`
)
for (g in group_order) {
  summary_tbl[[paste0("mean_PSMs_", g)]] <- mean_by_group(g)
  summary_tbl[[paste0("sd_PSMs_",   g)]] <- sd_by_group(g)
}
write_csv(summary_tbl, "group_summary.csv")
head(summary_tbl)
```

# Differential abundance — each treatment vs. control  →  `differential_abundance.csv`

Welch's t-test on raw PSM counts per protein, log2 fold-change with a +1
pseudocount, BH-FDR adjustment per comparison.

```{r diff_abundance}
PC <- 1
welch_one <- function(treat_vec, bc_vec) {
  if (sd(treat_vec) == 0 && sd(bc_vec) == 0) return(c(t = 0, p = 1))
  res <- tryCatch(t.test(treat_vec, bc_vec, var.equal = FALSE),
                  error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.null(res)) c(t = NA, p = 1) else c(t = unname(res$statistic),
                                            p = unname(res$p.value))
}

da_list <- list()
for (g in c("E", "F", "G")) {
  bc_mat <- psm_mat[, samples[["BC"]], drop = FALSE]
  tr_mat <- psm_mat[, samples[[g]],    drop = FALSE]
  log2fc <- log2(rowMeans(tr_mat) + PC) - log2(rowMeans(bc_mat) + PC)
  tp <- t(mapply(function(i) welch_one(tr_mat[i, ], bc_mat[i, ]),
                 i = seq_len(nrow(psm_mat))))
  da_list[[g]] <- tibble(
    protein     = clean$`Protein(s)`,
    description = clean$`Protein Description(s)`,
    comparison  = paste0(g, "_vs_BC"),
    mean_BC     = rowMeans(bc_mat),
    mean_treat  = rowMeans(tr_mat),
    log2FC      = log2fc,
    t_stat      = tp[, 1],
    p_value     = tp[, 2]
  ) %>%
    mutate(q_value = p.adjust(p_value, method = "BH"))
}
da <- bind_rows(da_list)
write_csv(da, "differential_abundance.csv")

da %>% group_by(comparison) %>%
  summarize(up   = sum(q_value < 0.05 & log2FC >  1, na.rm = TRUE),
            down = sum(q_value < 0.05 & log2FC < -1, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  print()
```

# Figure 4 — Volcano plot per comparison  →  `fig4_volcano.png`

```{r volcano}
da_plot <- da %>%
  mutate(neglog10p = -log10(pmax(p_value, 1e-300)),
         direction = case_when(
           q_value < 0.05 & log2FC >  1 ~ "Up",
           q_value < 0.05 & log2FC < -1 ~ "Down",
           TRUE                          ~ "ns"
         ),
         comparison = factor(comparison,
                             levels = c("E_vs_BC", "F_vs_BC", "G_vs_BC"),
                             labels = c("DNA Shield 1:1.5 vs Control",
                                        "DNA Shield 1:1.75 vs Control",
                                        "DNA Shield 1:2 vs Control")))

fig4 <- ggplot(da_plot, aes(log2FC, neglog10p, color = direction)) +
  geom_point(size = 0.7, alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = "dotted", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-1, 1),     linetype = "dotted", linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(Up = "#d62728", Down = "#1f77b4", ns = "lightgrey"),
                     name = NULL) +
  facet_wrap(~ comparison) +
  labs(title = "Differential abundance vs control",
       x = "log2 fold-change", y = "-log10 p-value") +
  theme_nature()
fig4
save_png(fig4, "fig4_volcano", w = 9, h = 3.5)
```

# Figure 5 — Heatmap of top variable proteins  →  `fig5_top_variable_heatmap.png`

```{r heatmap_top}
group_mean_mat <- sapply(group_order, mean_by_group)
rownames(group_mean_mat) <- clean$`Protein(s)`
colnames(group_mean_mat) <- group_labels[group_order]

top_n <- 50
top_idx <- order(apply(group_mean_mat, 1, sd), decreasing = TRUE)[seq_len(top_n)]
top_mat <- group_mean_mat[top_idx, ]
zmat    <- t(scale(t(top_mat)))

# Short row labels
desc_lookup <- setNames(clean$`Protein Description(s)`, clean$`Protein(s)`)
short <- str_split(desc_lookup[rownames(zmat)], ",", simplify = TRUE)[, 1]
short <- ifelse(nchar(short) > 55, paste0(substr(short, 1, 55), "…"), short)
rownames(zmat) <- short

png(file.path(fig_dir, "fig5_top_variable_heatmap.png"),
    width = 6, height = 9, units = "in", res = 600, type = "cairo")
pheatmap::pheatmap(
  zmat,
  cluster_rows = TRUE, cluster_cols = FALSE,
  color = colorRampPalette(rev(RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(11, "RdBu")))(100),
  fontsize_row = 5, fontsize_col = 8, fontsize = 7,
  main = sprintf("Top %d most variable proteins (z-score)", top_n),
  border_color = NA
)
invisible(dev.off())
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(fig_dir, "fig5_top_variable_heatmap.png"))
```

# Dose-response across the dilution gradient  →  `dose_response_trends.csv`

We test for monotonic trends across BC → E → F → G using Spearman
correlation between mean PSM count and an ordered dilution variable
(0, 1, 2, 3). A negative rho means the protein decreases with stronger
DNA Shield exposure; a positive rho means it increases.

```{r dose_response}
dilution_score <- c(BC = 0, E = 1, F = 2, G = 3)
trend_tbl <- tibble(
  protein     = clean$`Protein(s)`,
  description = clean$`Protein Description(s)`
) %>%
  mutate(spearman_rho = mapply(function(i) {
            x <- as.numeric(group_mean_mat[i, ])
            suppressWarnings(cor(x, dilution_score, method = "spearman"))
          }, seq_len(nrow(group_mean_mat))),
         delta_PSMs_G_minus_BC = group_mean_mat[, 4] - group_mean_mat[, 1])

write_csv(trend_tbl, "dose_response_trends.csv")

trend_tbl %>%
  arrange(spearman_rho) %>% head(10) %>% print()
trend_tbl %>%
  arrange(desc(spearman_rho)) %>% head(10) %>% print()
```

# Figure 8 — Per-peptide PSM scatter, control vs each dilution  →  `fig8_peptide_PSM_scatter.png` / `fig8_peptide_PSM_scatter_with_errorbars.png`

For each peptide, compare its mean PSM count in the control vs. its
mean PSM count in each treatment dilution (one panel per comparison).
Dots are colored by a **KEGG-style functional category** derived from
the primary protein description (regex panel below).

If you have a real KEGG-pathway annotation file (peptide-or-protein →
KEGG pathway), drop it into `kegg_annotations.tsv` in this folder
with columns `protein_id` and `pathway`, and replace the
`classify_kegg()` function below with a lookup against it. The rest
of the chunk is identical.

```{r peptide_scatter}
library(scales)

# Re-load peptides (already loaded in the qc chunk, but re-load
# defensively in case this chunk is run standalone)
pep <- read_tsv(peptides_file, show_col_types = FALSE)
pep_psm_cols <- paste0("PSM Count (", all_samples, ")")
pep <- pep %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(pep_psm_cols),
                ~ if (is.character(.x))
                    as.numeric(gsub(",", "", .x, fixed = TRUE)) else .x))

# Build a protein-id → description lookup from the proteins matrix
prot_desc <- raw %>%
  select(`Protein(s)`, `Protein Description(s)`) %>%
  separate_rows(`Protein(s)`, sep = ",") %>%
  mutate(`Protein(s)` = trimws(`Protein(s)`)) %>%
  distinct(`Protein(s)`, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  deframe()

# Pick first protein per peptide and look up its description
pep <- pep %>%
  mutate(
    first_prot = str_split_fixed(`Protein(s)`, ",", 2)[, 1] %>% trimws(),
    desc       = unname(prot_desc[first_prot])
  )

# KEGG-style classifier (regex panel; first match wins)
kegg_rules <- tribble(
  ~category,                ~regex,
  "Photosynthesis",         "(?i)rubisco|ribulose.{0,5}bisphosphate|photosystem|chloroplast|plastid|thylakoid|chlorophyll|petF|cbbX|pyrenoid|carbonic anhydrase",
  "Translation",            "(?i)ribosomal|ribosom|elongation factor|translation init|initiation factor|aminoacyl|tRNA synthetase|tRNA ligase|peptide chain release|EF-Tu|EF-G",
  "Transcription/DNA",      "(?i)RNA polymerase|RNAP|sigma factor|transcription|DNA polymerase|topoisomerase|gyrase|helicase|primase|nucleoid",
  "Chaperones / stress",    "(?i)chaperon|GroEL|GroES|DnaK|DnaJ|HtpG|heat shock|HSP|ClpX|ClpP|ClpA|trigger factor|FtsH|peptidyl-prolyl",
  "Energy / ATP synth.",    "(?i)ATP synthase|F0F1|ATP-?synth|cytochrome|NADH dehydrogenase|ubiquinol|succinate dehydrog|electron transport",
  "Central metabolism",     "(?i)citrate synthase|aconitase|isocitrate|2-oxoglut|alpha-?keto|succinyl|succinate|fumarat|malate dehydrog|GAPDH|glyceraldehyde|enolase|pyruvate kinase|hexokinase|phosphofructokin|aldolase|fructose|glucose-6|6-phosphate|TCA|tricarboxylic|glycolysis|pyruvate dehydrog|acetyl|fatty acid|lipid|amino acid|glutamate|glutamine|aspartate|alanine|serine|threonine|methionine|cyst|histid|lysine|arginin|tryptoph|phenylalan|tyrosin|leucin|isoleuc|valine|asparagine|glycine",
  "Nucleotide metab.",      "(?i)purine|pyrimidine|GMP|adenylate kinase|nucleoside-?diphosphate kinase|nucleotide|ribonucleot|deoxyribonucleot|adenosine|inosine",
  "Membrane / transport",   "(?i)porin|transporter|permease|channel|TonB|outer membrane|membrane protein|ABC transporter|secretion|TolQ|MotA|ExbB",
  "Hypothetical",           "(?i)hypothetical|predicted protein|uncharacterized|DUF\\d+|unnamed protein"
)
classify_kegg <- function(desc) {
  if (is.na(desc) || nchar(desc) == 0) return("Other")
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(kegg_rules))) {
    if (str_detect(desc, kegg_rules$regex[i])) return(kegg_rules$category[i])
  }
  "Other"
}
pep$category <- vapply(pep$desc, classify_kegg, character(1))

cat_order  <- c(kegg_rules$category, "Other")
cat_colors <- setNames(c("#2ca02c","#1f77b4","#17becf","#d62728","#ff7f0e",
                         "#9467bd","#e377c2","#bcbd22","#7f7f7f","#cccccc"),
                       cat_order)

# Per-group mean and SEM for each peptide
make_long <- function(samples_vec, group_name) {
  m <- pep %>% select(all_of(paste0("PSM Count (", samples_vec, ")"))) %>%
    rowwise() %>% summarize(
      mean = mean(c_across(everything())),
      sem  = sd(c_across(everything())) / sqrt(length(samples_vec)),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
  tibble(peptide = pep$`Peptide Sequence`,
         category = pep$category,
         group = group_name, mean = m$mean, sem = m$sem)
}

ctrl_long  <- make_long(samples[["BC"]], "Control")
treat_dfs  <- list(
  "DNA Shield 1:1.5"  = make_long(samples[["E"]], "DNA Shield 1:1.5"),
  "DNA Shield 1:1.75" = make_long(samples[["F"]], "DNA Shield 1:1.75"),
  "DNA Shield 1:2"    = make_long(samples[["G"]], "DNA Shield 1:2")
)

build_one <- function(treat_df, treat_label, show_errors) {
  d <- ctrl_long %>%
    rename(ctrl_mean = mean, ctrl_sem = sem) %>%
    select(-group) %>%
    inner_join(treat_df %>% rename(treat_mean = mean, treat_sem = sem) %>% select(-group),
               by = c("peptide", "category")) %>%
    filter(ctrl_mean + treat_mean > 0) %>%
    mutate(category = factor(category, levels = cat_order))

  ggplot(d, aes(x = ctrl_mean, y = treat_mean, color = category)) +
    {if (show_errors)
       geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = pmax(treat_mean - treat_sem, 0),
                         ymax = treat_mean + treat_sem),
                     width = 0, linewidth = 0.2, alpha = 0.3)} +
    {if (show_errors)
       geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = pmax(ctrl_mean - ctrl_sem, 0),
                          xmax = ctrl_mean + ctrl_sem),
                      height = 0, linewidth = 0.2, alpha = 0.3)} +
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed",
                color = "black", linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 0.7) +
    geom_point(size = 0.8, alpha = 0.7) +
    scale_color_manual(values = cat_colors, drop = FALSE,
                       name = "Functional category\n(KEGG-style)") +
    coord_equal() +
    labs(title = sprintf("%s vs Control", treat_label),
         x = "Mean PSM count — Control",
         y = sprintf("Mean PSM count — %s", treat_label)) +
    theme_nature() +
    guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3, alpha = 1)))
}

# Clean version (no error bars)
fig8_panels  <- lapply(names(treat_dfs), function(k)
                       build_one(treat_dfs[[k]], k, show_errors = FALSE))
fig8         <- patchwork::wrap_plots(fig8_panels, nrow = 1, guides = "collect")
ggsave("figures/fig8_peptide_PSM_scatter.png", fig8,
       width = 14, height = 4.5, dpi = 600, bg = "white", type = "cairo")

# Error-bars version
fig8b_panels <- lapply(names(treat_dfs), function(k)
                       build_one(treat_dfs[[k]], k, show_errors = TRUE))
fig8b        <- patchwork::wrap_plots(fig8b_panels, nrow = 1, guides = "collect")
ggsave("figures/fig8_peptide_PSM_scatter_with_errorbars.png", fig8b,
       width = 14, height = 4.5, dpi = 600, bg = "white", type = "cairo")

knitr::include_graphics("figures/fig8_peptide_PSM_scatter.png")
```

# Figure 9 — Top peptide modifications by PSM count  →  `fig9_modifications.png`

Loads the per-sample modification table (`bacteria_modificaiton_table.txt`
in the project root) — one row per integer-Da mass shift detected, one
column per sample with PSM counts. The top 12 modifications by total
PSM count are plotted as grouped bars per condition (mean ± SEM across
the three replicates). Common PTM identities are annotated by mass
shift using a Unimod-derived lookup.

```{r modifications}
mod_path <- "../bacteria_modificaiton_table.txt"   # one level up from Bacteria/
mods <- read_tsv(mod_path, show_col_types = FALSE)

# Map our group keys → modification-table column names
mod_cols <- list(
  BC = c("PSM count (bc1 mag)", "PSM count (bc2 mag)", "PSM count (bc3 mag)"),
  E  = c("PSM count (e1 mag)",  "PSM count (e2 mag)",  "PSM count (e3 mag)"),
  F  = c("PSM count (f1 mag)",  "PSM count (f2 mag)",  "PSM count (f3 mag)"),
  G  = c("PSM count (g1 mag)",  "PSM count (g2 mag)",  "PSM count (g3 mag)")
)

ptm_names <- c(
  `1`="Deamidation", `14`="Methylation", `15`="Reduction (-1H)",
  `16`="Oxidation", `17`="Loss of NH₃", `18`="Loss of H₂O",
  `22`="Sodium adduct", `28`="Dimethyl / Formyl", `32`="Dioxidation",
  `42`="Acetylation", `43`="Carbamylation", `56`="Succinylation",
  `57`="Carbamidomethyl (Cys)", `58`="Carboxymethyl",
  `71`="Propionamide", `79`="Phosphorylation", `80`="Phosphorylation",
  `114`="GG (di-glycyl, K-ubiq.)", `119`="Cysteinylation",
  `128`="Gln/Lys", `144`="HexNAc (glyco)",
  `156`="Aminoadipic semialdehyde", `162`="Hex (glyco)"
)

TOP_N <- 6
mods <- mods %>%
  mutate(total = rowSums(across(starts_with("PSM count")))) %>%
  arrange(desc(total)) %>%
  slice_head(n = TOP_N) %>%
  mutate(mod_label = ifelse(as.character(`Mod Mass`) %in% names(ptm_names),
    paste0("+", `Mod Mass`, "\n", ptm_names[as.character(`Mod Mass`)]),
    paste0("+", `Mod Mass`)))

mods_long <- imap_dfr(mod_cols, ~ {
  m  <- mods[, .x, drop = FALSE]
  tibble(
    mod_label = mods$mod_label,
    mod_mass  = mods$`Mod Mass`,
    group     = .y,
    mean_psm  = rowMeans(m),
    sem_psm   = apply(m, 1, sd) / sqrt(ncol(m))
  )
}) %>%
  mutate(mod_label = factor(mod_label, levels = mods$mod_label),
         group     = factor(group, levels = c("BC","E","F","G")))

fig9 <- ggplot(mods_long, aes(mod_label, mean_psm, fill = group)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), color = "black",
           linewidth = 0.3, width = 0.8) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = pmax(mean_psm - sem_psm, 0),
                    ymax = mean_psm + sem_psm),
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
                width = 0.3, linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = palette_cols, labels = group_labels, name = NULL) +
  labs(title = sprintf("Top %d peptide modifications by total PSM count", TOP_N),
       x = "Modification (Δ mass in Da)",
       y = "PSM count (mean ± SEM per replicate)") +
  theme_nature() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1, size = 7),
        legend.position = "top")

fig9
save_png(fig9, "fig9_modifications", w = 5.5, h = 4.5)
```

# Figure 11 — Per-protein NSAF scatter, control vs each dilution  →  `fig11_NSAF_scatter.png` / `fig11_NSAF_scatter_with_errorbars.png`

NSAF (Normalized Spectral Abundance Factor) is the per-protein PSM
count divided by protein length and then normalized so each sample
sums to a constant. Because NSAF is per-sample-normalized, comparing
means between treatments isolates **relative-abundance shifts** rather
than yield differences — exactly the right metric for an X-Y
treatment-comparison plot, and complementary to the peptide-level
PSM scatter in Figure 8.

```{r nsaf_scatter}
clean_nsaf <- read_csv("proteins_clean.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
clean_nsaf$category <- vapply(clean_nsaf$`Protein Description(s)`,
                              classify_kegg, character(1))

# Per-protein mean and SEM of NSAF, by group
nsaf_long <- function(samples_vec, group_name) {
  m <- clean_nsaf %>% select(all_of(paste0("NSAF (", samples_vec, ")")))
  tibble(
    protein  = clean_nsaf$`Protein(s)`,
    category = clean_nsaf$category,
    group    = group_name,
    mean     = rowMeans(m),
    sem      = apply(m, 1, sd) / sqrt(ncol(m))
  )
}
ctrl_long_n  <- nsaf_long(samples[["BC"]], "Control")
treat_dfs_n  <- list(
  "DNA Shield 1:1.5"  = nsaf_long(samples[["E"]], "DNA Shield 1:1.5"),
  "DNA Shield 1:1.75" = nsaf_long(samples[["F"]], "DNA Shield 1:1.75"),
  "DNA Shield 1:2"    = nsaf_long(samples[["G"]], "DNA Shield 1:2")
)

build_nsaf <- function(treat_df, treat_label, show_errors) {
  d <- ctrl_long_n %>%
    rename(ctrl_mean = mean, ctrl_sem = sem) %>%
    select(-group) %>%
    inner_join(treat_df %>% rename(treat_mean = mean, treat_sem = sem) %>% select(-group),
               by = c("protein", "category")) %>%
    filter(ctrl_mean > 0, treat_mean > 0) %>%   # both > 0 required for log10
    mutate(
      # Pre-transform to log10 — axes show actual log10 values (e.g. -4, -3, -2)
      lx      = log10(ctrl_mean),
      ly      = log10(treat_mean),
      # Error bars in log10 space: asymmetric — upper/lower computed from raw then logged
      eb_ymin = log10(pmax(treat_mean - treat_sem, treat_mean * 0.01)),
      eb_ymax = log10(treat_mean + treat_sem),
      eb_xmin = log10(pmax(ctrl_mean  - ctrl_sem,  ctrl_mean  * 0.01)),
      eb_xmax = log10(ctrl_mean  + ctrl_sem)
    )

  # 2D kernel density in log10 space (matches the plotted axes)
  dens2d    <- MASS::kde2d(d$lx, d$ly, n = 200)
  ix        <- findInterval(d$lx, dens2d$x)
  iy        <- findInterval(d$ly, dens2d$y)
  d$density <- dens2d$z[cbind(ix, iy)]

  ggplot(d, aes(x = lx, y = ly)) +
    {if (show_errors)
       geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = eb_ymin, ymax = eb_ymax),
                     width = 0, linewidth = 0.2, alpha = 0.25, color = "grey50")} +
    {if (show_errors)
       geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = eb_xmin, xmax = eb_xmax),
                      height = 0, linewidth = 0.2, alpha = 0.25, color = "grey50")} +
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed",
                color = "grey20", linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 0.7) +
    geom_point(aes(color = density), size = 1.0, alpha = 0.85) +
    scale_color_viridis_c(
      option    = "plasma",   # purple (sparse) -> yellow (dense)
      direction = 1,
      name      = "Density"
    ) +
    coord_equal() +
    labs(title = sprintf("%s vs Control", treat_label),
         x     = "log10(Mean NSAF) — Control",
         y     = sprintf("log10(Mean NSAF) — %s", treat_label)) +
    theme_nature()
}

fig11_panels  <- lapply(names(treat_dfs_n),
                        function(k) build_nsaf(treat_dfs_n[[k]], k, FALSE))
fig11         <- patchwork::wrap_plots(fig11_panels, nrow = 1, guides = "collect")
ggsave("figures/fig11_NSAF_scatter.png", fig11,
       width = 14, height = 4.5, dpi = 600, bg = "white", type = "cairo")

fig11b_panels <- lapply(names(treat_dfs_n),
                        function(k) build_nsaf(treat_dfs_n[[k]], k, TRUE))
fig11b        <- patchwork::wrap_plots(fig11b_panels, nrow = 1, guides = "collect")
ggsave("figures/fig11_NSAF_scatter_with_errorbars.png", fig11b,
       width = 14, height = 4.5, dpi = 600, bg = "white", type = "cairo")

knitr::include_graphics("figures/fig11_NSAF_scatter.png")
```

# Figure 10 — Peptide identification overlap across conditions (4-way Venn)  →  `fig10_peptide_venn.png`

A peptide is counted as "present in a group" if it has PSM Count > 0 in
≥ 2 of the 3 replicates of that group (a simple presence-call rule that
filters single-replicate noise). The 4-way Venn shows all 15 non-empty
intersection regions with both raw count and percent of the total
union (i.e., percent of all peptides reliably detected in any group).

```{r peptide_overlap}
# install.packages("ggVennDiagram") if missing
library(ggVennDiagram)

make_present_set <- function(samples_vec) {
  cols <- paste0("PSM Count (", samples_vec, ")")
  pres <- rowSums(pep[, cols] > 0) >= 2
  unique(as.character(pep$`Peptide Sequence`[pres]))
}
venn_sets <- list(
  "Control"           = make_present_set(samples[["BC"]]),
  "DNA Shield 1:1.5"  = make_present_set(samples[["E"]]),
  "DNA Shield 1:1.75" = make_present_set(samples[["F"]]),
  "DNA Shield 1:2"    = make_present_set(samples[["G"]])
)
total_union <- length(unique(unlist(venn_sets)))

# label = "both" prints count and percent in each region
fig10 <- ggVennDiagram(
  venn_sets,
  label = "both", label_size = 3,
  label_alpha = 0,
  edge_color = "black", edge_size = 0.5,
  set_size = 4
) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "#1B4F72", guide = "none") +
  coord_fixed(clip = "off") +
  labs(title = "Peptide identifications shared across conditions",
       subtitle = sprintf("(present in ≥ 2 of 3 replicates per group; total union = %s peptides)",
                          format(total_union, big.mark = ","))) +
  theme_void(base_size = 9) +
  theme(plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5))

fig10
save_png(fig10, "fig10_peptide_venn", w = 7.5, h = 5.5)
```

# Singleton-peptide filter — alternative versions of fig 1, 3, 7, 10  →  `figures/peptide_filtered/*.png`

This block re-renders four key figures (sample QC, sample correlation,
NMDS, peptide Venn) after applying a **≥ 2 PSMs per peptide** filter —
i.e., dropping "one-hit-wonder" peptides whose total PSM count across
all samples is 1. Filter removes 6,054 / 33,221 peptides (~18% of the
bacteria set). The filtered versions sit alongside the unfiltered
originals in `figures/peptide_filtered/`.

```{r peptide_filtered}
library(vegan)
# Re-load + filter
pep_full <- read_tsv(peptides_file, show_col_types = FALSE)
pep_cols <- paste0("PSM Count (", all_samples, ")")
pep_full <- pep_full %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(pep_cols),
                ~ if (is.character(.x))
                    as.numeric(gsub(",", "", .x, fixed = TRUE)) else .x))
pep_full[is.na(pep_full)] <- 0
pep_filt <- pep_full %>% mutate(total_psm = rowSums(across(all_of(pep_cols)))) %>%
                        filter(total_psm >= 2)

cat(sprintf("Kept %d / %d peptides (filtered out %d singletons, %.1f%%)\n",
            nrow(pep_filt), nrow(pep_full),
            nrow(pep_full) - nrow(pep_filt),
            (nrow(pep_full) - nrow(pep_filt)) / nrow(pep_full) * 100))

dir.create("figures/peptide_filtered", showWarnings = FALSE)

# fig1 (filtered) — sample QC
qc_f <- tibble(
  sample = all_samples,
  group  = rep(group_order, each = 3),
  total_PSMs = colSums(pep_filt[, pep_cols]),
  n_peptides = colSums(pep_filt[, pep_cols] > 0)
) %>% mutate(group = factor(group, levels = group_order))
qc_grp_f <- qc_f %>% group_by(group) %>%
  summarize(psm_mean = mean(total_PSMs), psm_sem = sd(total_PSMs)/sqrt(n()),
            pep_mean = mean(n_peptides), pep_sem = sd(n_peptides)/sqrt(n()),
            .groups = "drop")
p1f <- ggplot(qc_grp_f, aes(group, psm_mean, fill = group)) +
  geom_col(color = "black", linewidth = 0.4, width = 0.7) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = psm_mean - psm_sem, ymax = psm_mean + psm_sem),
                width = 0.18, linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_jitter(data = qc_f, aes(group, total_PSMs),
              width = 0.10, shape = 21, fill = "white",
              color = "black", stroke = 0.4, size = 1.6, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  geom_signif(data = qc_f, aes(group, total_PSMs),
              comparisons = sig_comparisons, test = "t.test",
              test.args = list(var.equal = FALSE),
              map_signif_level = TRUE, step_increase = 0.08,
              tip_length = 0.01, vjust = 0.2, textsize = 2.6, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = palette_cols, guide = "none") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = group_labels) +
  labs(title = "a  Total PSMs per sample (singletons removed)",
       x = NULL, y = "Total PSMs (mean ± SEM)") +
  theme_nature() + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 20, hjust = 1))
p2f <- p1f %+% aes(y = pep_mean) +
  geom_signif(data = qc_f, aes(group, n_peptides),
              comparisons = sig_comparisons, test = "t.test",
              test.args = list(var.equal = FALSE),
              map_signif_level = TRUE, step_increase = 0.08,
              tip_length = 0.01, vjust = 0.2, textsize = 2.6, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "b  Peptides detected (PSM > 0; singletons removed)",
       y = "Peptides detected (mean ± SEM)")
fig1_filt <- p1f + p2f
ggsave("figures/peptide_filtered/fig1_sample_QC.png", fig1_filt,
       width = 7, height = 4, dpi = 600, bg = "white", type = "cairo")

# fig3 (filtered) — sample correlation on the per-peptide matrix
mat_pep <- log2(as.matrix(pep_filt[, pep_cols]) + 1)
colnames(mat_pep) <- all_samples
corr_f <- cor(mat_pep, method = "pearson")
png("figures/peptide_filtered/fig3_sample_correlation.png",
    width = 5, height = 4.5, units = "in", res = 600, type = "cairo")
pheatmap::pheatmap(corr_f, display_numbers = TRUE, number_format = "%.2f",
  color = viridis::viridis(100), fontsize = 7, fontsize_number = 5,
  main = "Sample correlation (log2 PSM, peptide-level, singletons removed)",
  border_color = NA)
invisible(dev.off())

# fig7 (filtered) — NMDS Bray-Curtis on the per-peptide matrix
set.seed(42)
nmds_f <- metaMDS(t(pep_filt[, pep_cols]), distance = "bray", k = 2,
                  trymax = 50, autotransform = FALSE, trace = FALSE)
nmds_df_f <- as_tibble(nmds_f$points, rownames = "sample") %>%
  mutate(group = rep(group_order, each = 3),
         group = factor(group, levels = group_order))
fig7_filt <- ggplot(nmds_df_f, aes(MDS1, MDS2, fill = group)) +
  geom_point(size = 3.2, shape = 21, color = "black", stroke = 0.4) +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = sample), size = 2.4, box.padding = 0.25) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = palette_cols, labels = group_labels, name = NULL) +
  labs(title = sprintf("NMDS (Bray-Curtis, peptide-level, singletons removed) — stress = %.3f",
                       nmds_f$stress),
       x = "NMDS 1", y = "NMDS 2") +
  theme_nature()
ggsave("figures/peptide_filtered/fig7_NMDS.png", fig7_filt,
       width = 4.5, height = 3.5, dpi = 600, bg = "white", type = "cairo")

# fig10 (filtered) — 4-way Venn
library(ggVennDiagram)
make_present_set_f <- function(samples_vec) {
  cols <- paste0("PSM Count (", samples_vec, ")")
  pres <- rowSums(pep_filt[, cols] > 0) >= 2
  unique(as.character(pep_filt$`Peptide Sequence`[pres]))
}
venn_sets_f <- list(
  "Control"           = make_present_set_f(samples[["BC"]]),
  "DNA Shield 1:1.5"  = make_present_set_f(samples[["E"]]),
  "DNA Shield 1:1.75" = make_present_set_f(samples[["F"]]),
  "DNA Shield 1:2"    = make_present_set_f(samples[["G"]])
)
total_union_f <- length(unique(unlist(venn_sets_f)))
fig10_filt <- ggVennDiagram(venn_sets_f, label = "both", label_size = 3,
                            label_alpha = 0, edge_color = "black",
                            edge_size = 0.5, set_size = 4) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "#1B4F72", guide = "none") +
  coord_fixed(clip = "off") +
  labs(title = "Peptide overlap (singletons removed)",
       subtitle = sprintf("≥ 2 of 3 reps per group; union = %s peptides",
                          format(total_union_f, big.mark = ","))) +
  theme_void(base_size = 9) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
ggsave("figures/peptide_filtered/fig10_peptide_venn.png", fig10_filt,
       width = 7.5, height = 5.5, dpi = 600, bg = "white", type = "cairo")
```

# Session info

```{r session_info}
sessionInfo()
```
